briefs.title: BC Pharmacies Banned from Charging Patients for Fully Covered Drugs Starting March 1
briefs.summary: BC is updating its drug plan rules to ban pharmacies from billing patients for drugs already fully covered by a government drug plan, and insulin is now subject to standard drug pricing rules. A new drug plan called Plan NP is also created for all BC medicare (public health insurance) beneficiaries.
briefs.action_required: Review your pharmacy billing practices to ensure no covered drugs are charged to patients starting March 1, 2026.

key_dates.label: BC drug plan amendments (Plan NP, insulin pricing, billing ban) take effect
